history.content:
<thought>
I can see walls on column C (#) and lava in rows 04 and 06, including lava directly ahead if I face up. The only safe open spaces near me are to the left or right of my current position; to avoid the lava band, I should turn right to head into the open floor region.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible yet, but I can see many lava cells (L) to the left side. From my current position (row 07, column D) I should avoid moving into lava; the cell directly in front depends on my current facing, but turning right would move away from the lava columns on the left and toward the open floor at column D/E where there are '.' cells. I'll turn right to seek a safer forward path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m now facing upward (^) near the bottom row 07. Directly above me (row 06, same column D) is a wall (#) because row 06 is mostly all #. Since forward is blocked, I should turn right to look for an open neighboring cell that isn’t a wall.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing up. The cell in front (row 06 column D) is unknown (?), not a wall, so moving forward is potentially safe. I’ll try MOVE_FORWARD to progress upward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I attempted to move forward, but my position didn’t change (still shows ^ at row 07 column D). That means the forward cell is blocked (likely a wall at row 06 column D). To find an open route, I’ll turn right to inspect the neighboring cell to my right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can now see I’m at row 07 column D facing up. The cell in front (row 06 column D) is `L` lava, so I must not move forward. To find a safe path, I’ll turn left to face the cell at my left (row 07 column C), which appears to be `#` wall, so instead I’ll turn right to face east (row 07 column E) which is `.` and safe.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing up/right so that the cell directly in front is row 06 column D, which is `.` (safe). I should move forward to approach the open corridor while avoiding any `L` lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
In front of me is row 07 column D currently showing `^` at row 07 col D, and the cell ahead (row 06 col D) is `.`. I can move forward safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible yet. I’m facing the bottom-center `^` at row 07 column D with front cell at row 06 column D, which is `.` (safe). I should advance toward the open area.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m now at row 07 column D and facing upward (the `^` is on row 07 col D). The cell directly in front (row 06 col D) is `.` so it’s safe to move forward toward the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 06 column D facing up; the cell in front (row 05 column D) is `.`. Moving forward keeps me in the safe area toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing up. The cell in front (row 06 column D) is `.` but in this view row 06 column D is occupied by `.` (safe), and row 05 is all `#` so the only way is to keep moving forward. Since the immediate cell ahead is safe, I’ll move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column A/B? The separator shows my position at row 07 column D (^) with walls directly above at row 06 are all `#` (row 06 columns A–E). So I cannot move forward upward. To look for a path around the wall, I’ll turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column D facing right. The cell in front (row 07 column E) is `.` so it’s safe to move into. I’ll move forward to progress around the wall.
</thought>
<action>

<thought>

<action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>

<thought>
I appear to be stuck: the map still shows my `^` at row 07 column D, with `.` cells in the view to the right (E and F) but no change in position. Since moving forward didn't shift me, I will adjust orientation by turning right to align toward the open spaces.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT

<thought>
The goal is not visible yet, but I can see I’m in column D of this local view with walls at column C (`#`). Lava (`L`) occupies column E in most rows, so moving forward into E is dangerous. The safer open cells are to my left (column D) vs right (column E). Since forward direction is unknown, I will turn right to face away from the lava column E before moving.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I can now see the goal `G` at row 01 column D, and my position `^` is at row 07 column D (same column). The cells directly above are: row 06 column D is `.` (safe), row 05 column D is `.` (safe), row 04 column D is `.` (safe). However, row 03 column D is `.` and row 02 column D is `.` and row 01 column D is `G`. To move toward it, I need to face upward from my current position. I’m currently facing unknown; I will turn left until forward is upward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal `G` is at row 01 column D. I’m at row 07 column D and can move straight upward along column D: row 06 column D is `.`, row 05 column D is `.`, row 04 column D is `.`, row 03 column D is `.`, row 02 column D is `.`, and row 01 column D is `G`. I should ensure I face upward; right now the view shows my position `^` in the bottom row aligned with column D, and after turning left earlier I should be facing up. So I will move forward into row 06 column D.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m now at row 06 column D (cell above was clear before, and the goal at row 01 column D is still blocked only by lava not on column D). In the current view, the cell directly forward (row 05 column D) is `.` and not lava, so I can continue moving upward toward the goal.

<action>
MOVE_FORWARD

<thought>

<thought>
